export default "
\n
\n
\n Angular {{ releaseLine.angular }} · @stackline/loading {{ releaseLine.coreVersion }}\n

@stackline/angular-loading

\n

\n A maintained Angular wrapper around the Stackline loading toolkit. Use a component for direct templates,\n a directive for cards and buttons, and a service for fullscreen or ad-hoc loading states.\n

\n
\n
\n Component API\n Render loaders declaratively or wrap projected content.\n
\n
\n Directive API\n Attach loading behavior to cards, tables, and buttons.\n
\n
\n Service API\n Create fullscreen or programmatic loaders when the template is not enough.\n
\n
\n Core fidelity\n The wrapper passes through the same modern variants and anti-flicker options.\n
\n
\n
\n See demos\n README\n
\n
\n\n
\n

\n          
Core wrapper demos
\n          
This Angular line covers the most practical loading patterns for enterprise apps and SaaS screens.
\n        
\n\n        
\n          
\n            
Standalone component
\n            
Use the component directly when the loader itself is the main thing you want to render.
\n            
\n              
\n                \n              
\n              
\n            
\n            
\n              \n            
\n          
\n\n          
\n            
Directive overlay on a card
\n            
The directive is the best fit for cards, panels, and existing containers.
\n

\n          
\n\n          
\n            
Button loading state
\n            
The directive also works well on buttons with compact inline variants and no visible label.
\n            
\n              \n            
\n          
\n\n          
\n            
Service-driven fullscreen
\n            
When the loader must be triggered imperatively, use the Angular service and the core instance underneath.

API reference
\n          
The wrapper is intentionally thin. Angular gives you structure; the core library supplies the variants and behavior.
